- Algebra has already deployed an AI-powered chatbot on its student portal for about a year, helping students with administrative and academic questions such as class schedules, exam locations, available micro-qualifications, and enrollment details. The chatbot, built in-house using an unnamed component, is widely used and appreciated by students. In parallel, Algebra is piloting a similar chatbot within its Learning Management System (LMS) to act as a mentor bot, answering questions about course content and supporting students during self-study. This initiative involves migrating course materials from PDFs into a structured, web-friendly format that the chatbot can access and reference. The LMS chatbot is designed to be context-aware and limited to the materials relevant to each student’s courses. While teachers are also exploring how to adapt exams and assessments to allow ethical AI usage, the chatbot itself is not currently used for exams or grading. The technical approach appears to use a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pattern, where queries are matched against institutional documents before generating an AI-assisted response. This initiative demonstrates Algebra’s effort to integrate AI thoughtfully into the student experience, both administratively and academically, while maintaining academic integrity and enhancing learning outcomes.

column12:
AI para todos Mexico or The AI for All Mexico Program, promoted by CompuEducación, has proven to be a significant milestone in the adoption and development of artificial intelligence (AI) skills in Mexico. This free program has offered a series of webinars and specialized courses designed to transform the personal and professional lives of participants, regardless of their prior knowledge level.

The alliance and alignment of the AI for All Mexico Program with Microsoft's global objectives is evident, as both seek to democratize access to knowledge in artificial intelligence and prepare society for a more inclusive digital future. Microsoft, as a leader in technological innovation, actively promotes digital skills training and the ethical integration of AI in multiple sectors. This project in Mexico reflects those same principles, offering free, high-quality training that empowers people of all profiles, closing knowledge gaps, and facilitating the responsible adoption of AI. In this way, progress is being made in building a robust, diverse technological ecosystem prepared to face the challenges and opportunities presented by the artificial intelligence revolution.

column4:
Is your organization doing any type of AI consulting? If so, what are you working on?
Yes, our organization offers AI consulting services focused on helping companies navigate digital transformation and integrate AI tools into their operations. We provide two main types of consultations:

Educational Consulting – This involves identifying which AI tools and knowledge areas would be most beneficial for a company’s employees. We then support them with tailored training and learning resources to build the necessary skills. Focused on the big picture, we walk them through the whole process no matter their starting point.

Implementational Consulting – In this phase, we go beyond education to actively design and implement AI solutions based on Microsoft services, including security features and custom AI agents. These solutions are tailored to streamline workflows, enhance decision-making, and improve operational efficiency across various business environments.

Our goal is to make AI both accessible and impactful for organizations, no matter where they are in their digital journey.



Is there any important information about AI within your organization that has not been addressed?
Yes, there are a few important aspects worth highlighting. Most notably, we are highly motivated to lead and implement LLPA’s global initiatives in the Balkans and Adriatic region. We see significant potential in this part of Europe and are fully prepared to take the lead in driving these efforts forward.

In addition, we prioritize staying informed about emerging AI trends and closely monitor developments across industries and training providers. This helps us remain competitive and responsive to market needs.

While we are creative in our approach to clients, we occasionally face challenges in setting clear priorities and balancing development with sales. With strategic guidance and support in identifying focus areas, we believe we could accelerate our growth and expand our impact.

column6: We offer a comprehensive suite of educational and consulting services to support AI adoption and digital transformation.,"These include webinars, panel discussions, half-day or one-day workshops, multi-day or multi-month courses, and academies which are up to ten months long. We also organise fairs/ conferences.","In addition, we provide strategic consulting and hands-on implementation of AI solutions, including the development of custom AI agents.","Even more, we have developed a video platform featuring over 50 expert-led sessions exploring AI applications across various industries and in multiple languages."

column9: "Our most common AI training requests currently center around Microsoft technologies, with our most popular course being Empower Your Workforce with Copilot for Microsoft 365 Use Cases.","Since the beginning of the year, we have trained approximately 1,000 participants in this program.","Courses on ChatGPT prompting, AI in finance, and AI in HR are also gaining traction.","Additionally, our collaboration with the Macedonian Chamber of Commerce has significantly boosted interest in AI for accountants, which is experiencing rapid growth in demand."
column10: One of the biggest challenges we face—both internally and with our clients—is the lack of a clear methodology for mapping AI opportunities within organizations.,"Without a structured approach, it’s difficult to identify where and how AI can be effectively introduced and implemented.","Additionally, many companies struggle with keeping up with the rapid pace of AI development, which makes it hard for us to establish a consistent support system that meets their evolving needs.",A major gap we’ve identified is the absence of industry benchmarks—both for AI adoption and for the use of specific tools.,"Having access to these benchmarks would help us guide our clients more effectively, certify our trainers, and develop more targeted learning paths","We’ve begun creating AI learning paths tailored to specific industries, professions, and roles, ensuring that business leaders and technical staff receive content suited to their responsibilities.","However, we aim to expand into more industries and would benefit greatly from insights into how AI is being implemented across different sectors.","Finally, we are committed to increasing AI awareness through initiatives like our 30for30 platform, but we are also keen to learn how other regions and industries are successfully raising awareness and driving adoption."
column12:
Yes, our organization is currently developing and delivering several major AI projects and initiatives. These represent some of our largest and most impactful efforts:

AI National Skills Initiative (AI NSI): Implemented across North Macedonia, Serbia, Montenegro, Kosovo, and Albania, this initiative focuses on building foundational AI skills and awareness in the region.

30 for 30 Platform: A regional AI awareness and education campaign active in the same countries, offering 30 expert-led videos on AI applications across industries.

Partnerships with Key Institutions: We are collaborating with the Macedonian Chamber of Commerce and the Institute for Chartered Accountants to deliver specialized AI training for professionals, including accountants.

Microsoft AI Training Programs: Delivered in Moldova, Belgium, North Macedonia, and the USA, these programs have reached over 300 participants, focusing on practical AI applications in business environments.

Agency for Employment in North Macedonia: We provided a Generative AI course to all participants, with a portion continuing into our AI Academy for deeper specialization.

AI Essentials on AWS Campaign: Targeted at AWS partners from six countries in the Adriatic region and their global affiliates. This initiative offers subsidized one-day training focused on Generative AI concepts, prompt engineering, and industry-specific use cases.

CANSEE Partnership: Our Serbian branch partnered with the Canadian-Serbian Business Association (CANSEE) to deliver AI-related workshops and participate in their “AI, Leadership and Entrepreneurship – Shaping the Future Together” conference.

Corporate AI Training Initiatives: We supported Raiffeisen Bank’s skilling initiative in Kosovo and Albania, as well as NLB Bank and IPKO’s AI programs in Kosovo. These recent initiatives have already engaged over 150 participants.

In addition to these projects, we regularly deliver AI training programs for a wide range of tech companies across the region, helping drive adoption and practical implementation of AI technologies.
column17:
We currently offer AI specializations in several areas, including AI in finance, AI in HR, AI agents, and Microsoft technologies. Additionally, we have developed AI modules tailored to each of our 15 academies, covering fields such as social media, interior, graphic and industrial design, game development, and project management among others.

However, one area where we see a clear gap is AI for IT professionals, particularly in non-vendor-based, open-source technologies. Strengthening our offerings in this space is a priority, especially as we aim to build stronger relationships with IT companies and tech experts.

We are also interested in gaining deeper insights into AI business mapping and implementation strategies. As we tailor our training and consulting services to the software ecosystems used by our clients, expanding our expertise in this area would allow us to deliver even more targeted and impactful solutions.
column18:
Yes, there are several important developments worth mentioning. Most notably, we have taken a leading role in advancing AI adoption in North Macedonia and the broader Adriatic region. We launched the AI National Skills Initiative (AI NSI) with full support from the Ministry for Digital Transformation, and we organized the first AI conference in the country, which was attended by President Stevo Pendarovski.

We have also partnered with the Employment Agency of the Republic of North Macedonia for several consecutive years to deliver AI training programs for unemployed individuals. Through our AI Academy, many participants have gained practical skills and transitioned into AI-related careers. Those who chose different career paths also attended AI courses which were tailored to their specific industry.

While there are currently no national AI regulations, we closely follow EU guidelines, as North Macedonia is an EU candidate country. This ensures our work remains aligned with broader European standards and best practices.
column20: Yes, we would be very interested.
column21:
AI adoption in North Macedonia and the region is steadily growing, with increasing interest from both individuals and organizations. There are currently no significant cultural concerns or resistance regarding AI. On the contrary, we—along with other key players in the market—are actively promoting AI through education, training, and awareness campaigns.

As a result, a growing number of professionals and everyday users are integrating AI into both their personal and professional activities. The general sentiment is open, creating a favorable environment for further adoption and innovation.
column22: We actively collaborate with various organizations on AI initiatives, though not with local universities. For example, we participated in Startup Macedonia’s AI in Tech initiative, which brought together key players in the local tech ecosystem.
